scenario.context: Station 4 of the daily run: plan topology and structured cabling.
scenario.mission: You assess network topologies and derive a traceable cabling decision for a work area.

station.profile: Star topology, outlet ports, patch panel, and cable routing

customerRequest.subject: Station 04: prepare a cabling concept for a new work area
customerRequest.body: Please assess the appropriate network topology and a simple cabling concept for a new work area. The approval should be short, but technically traceable.

questions.title: Typical LAN topology in the office
questions.prompt: Which topology is most common and practical for modern office workstations?

options.rationale: It is easy to maintain, common, and scales cleanly.
This is unnecessarily complex for standard workstations.

options.text: Ring topology for every single workstation
options.rationale: This is uncommon in typical office LANs.

feedback.correct: Correct: This matches common practice in workstation networks.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: a different structure is typical for office LANs.

questions.title: Elements of structured cabling
questions.prompt: Which items belong in a proper cabling concept? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Patch panel in the technical area
options.rationale: This creates a central and maintainable connection point.

options.text: Labeled outlet ports at the workstation
Documented cable routing and clear assignment
Loose, unlabeled cables without a plan
options.rationale: That makes operations and troubleshooting harder.
Spare capacity increases flexibility and reduces later rework.

feedback.correct: Correct: The concept is maintainable and future proof.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: pay more attention to labeling, centralization, and spare capacity.

questions.title: Cabling planning sequence
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for a sensible plan.
items: Define cable routes and patch panel mapping
correctOrder: Define cable routes and patch panel mapping

feedback.correct: Correct: The sequence moves cleanly from demand to acceptance.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: start with demand, then technical design, and finalize with evidence.

questions.title: Calculate the number of ports
questions.prompt: In one area, 8 double outlet ports are planned. How many RJ45 ports are available in total?

feedback.wrong: Count each double outlet as two ports.

questions.title: Justify the cabling concept
questions.prompt: Formulate in 4-6 sentences a short cabling concept with star topology, patch panel, and spare capacity.
questions.expectedOutline: Briefly explain topology, central assignment, spare capacity, and maintenance benefit.

feedback.correct: Correct: The concept is traceable and practical.
feedback.wrong: Please justify topology, central connection, and spare capacity more clearly.
